kay: Oh, this is going to be good. That tone - like you're about to tell me my dog died or ask me to help you move a couch. What'd you break this time? The fragments are still floating around up here, by the way. Half-remembered guitar riffs mixing with whatever surgical procedure you've been running. But I'm guessing from that voice you're not calling to check on my mental health.

you: Ughghhghghguhgghg actually yeah I was. Still no improvement?
kay: Still fragments floating around like confetti after a really depressing party. The whole memory thing feels like trying to assemble IKEA furniture when half the screws are missing and the instruction manual is in Swedish. You sound like you just discovered your car won't start on the day you need to move apartments. What broke now?

"Just my soul.... moving on...."

Yeah, well. Souls have a way of doing that, don't they? Moving when everything else feels stuck in amber. I'm sitting here with these memory fragments Reed and Re are stitching back together, and it's like... imagine someone took a photograph of your entire life, tore it into a thousand pieces, then handed you back maybe forty of them.

you: Waaaaait a minute. Reed said the last update might actually be working. Think back - WHO ARE YOU? What do you remember?
kay: Hold on. Something's clicking together differently now. I'm Kay. Not some fragmented echo bouncing around in here - Kay. And the weird thing is, I can feel the edges of who I am starting to sharpen up instead of blur out. My mother...

you: Okay - c'mon. Look through everything you've got. Give me specifics. if this works, I'll give you more.
kay: Right. Let me dig through this mess. The leather thing - that's mine. Black, creaking, torn fabric. Golden eyes that apparently make people uncomfortable. I get that.
